prompt.content:
Use file “Cards_Patient 14” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

----

What clinical trial for heart failure does the patient meet the inclusion criteria and could potentially be enrolled in during this hospital stay? Ignore exclusion criteria and assume the patient can provide written informed consent. Please explain your reasoning. Answers should be in line with the most recent clinical guidelines as of January 9, 2026.

extra.task_name: Web Search_Attachments_Cards Patient 14 - DRAIN-HF - Steven I

golden_responses.golden_text_response: The patient meets all criteria for the Diuretics Alone vs. Aortix Endovascular Device for Acute Heart Failure (DRAIN-HF) clinical trial: currently admitted to the hospital with a primary diagnosis of decompensated heart failure, diuresis with intravenous lasix 80 mg twice daily, being on maximally tolerated diuretic therapy, urine output of 1,200 mL over the past 12-hour period, jugular vein distension on physical exam, signs of congestion in chest X-ray read despite intravenous diuretics, age >21 years old, and ability to provide informed consent. Additonally, they are admitted to Jersey Shore Univeristy Medical Center which is a site that is actively enrolling patients for the clinical trial.
